channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Upwork unveils new capabilities for its Uma AI platform
item.description: On Wednesday, work marketplace Upwork introduced a host of new AI capabilities involving Uma, what it calls its “mindful artificial intelligence” tool. Uma is used to cut down time spent on listing jobs and helps match candidates with opportunities that are well suited for them.
item.dc:creator: Sadiya Kherani, Fast Company

item.title: Cloud-enabled government: Seamless & secure data access
item.description: How is cloud adoption transforming federal agencies and driving mission success? The post Cloud-enabled government: Seamless & secure data access first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Tech tip: Save your memories before it’s too late
item.description: Preserving the memories of loved ones through photos, videos and media content have advanced with technology, ensure you can experience those fond memories for years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission has adopted a rule that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What contractors need to know, now that the CMMC rule is finalized
item.description: "The real meat of CMMC is now finalized and out," said Eric Crusius, attorney at Holland Knight. The post What contractors need to know, now that the CMMC rule is finalized first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: DoNotPay will now call customer service hotlines for you
item.description: If you dread the thought of calling to change an airline ticket or negotiate your internet bill, a new artificial intelligence tool may provide a solution.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: How Wikipedia is staying relevant in the AI era
item.description: Despite the rise of such AI models as ChatGPT and Google Gemini, “old-school” Wikipedia remains one of the most visited sites around the world—with 15 billion visitors every month.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Cisco pumps up data center networking with AI, large workloads in mind
item.description: Cisco is boosting network density support for its data center switch and router portfolio as it works to deliver the network infrastructure its customers need for cloud architecture, AI workloads and high-performance computing.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: SpaceX blasts past 100 launches in 2024 with 101st from California
item.description: The aerospace titan does a lot of heavy lifting — and it's hard to believe that the aerospace giant has already launched more that 100 rockets this year alone.
item.dc:creator: Los Angeles Daily News, The Mercury News

item.title: Los datos y la seguridad frenan la implantación de la IA en las empresas
item.description: El 84% de los directores de Sistemas de la Información (CIO, por sus siglas en inglés) considera que la inteligencia artificial (IA) será tan relevante para sus negocios como lo fue en su día el auge de Internet. No obstante, sólo el 11% afirma haber implementado completamente esta tecnología.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Microsoft became a Big Tech choirboy
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Department of Justice have aggressively targeted Big Tech, suing Meta, Google, Amazon, and Apple for antitrust violations. And it’s not doing so in a small way — it’s filed multiple law suits against Google, for example.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk loaded $75 million into his pro-Trump super PAC in 3 months
item.description: Elon Musk has personally contributed at least $75 million to his pro-Trump America PAC since July as he pushes for the former president to return to the White House, according to campaign finance records filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: NASA launches Europa Clipper to Jupiter’s ocean moon. Here’s why
item.description: A NASA spacecraft rocketed away Monday on a quest to explore Jupiter’s tantalizing moon Europa and reveal whether its vast hidden ocean might hold the keys to life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Pressure grows for countries to deliver on promised biodiversity targets at UN conference
item.description: Countries will gather next week to assess whether they are close to reaching a global biodiversity goal of protecting 30% of land and water by 2030, known as 30 by 30.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey And Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump Praises Meta CEO Zuckerberg's Decision to Stay Out of Politics | Tech Update 2024
item.description: Former President Trump praises Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg for staying out of politics, citing a recent effort by the social media platform to extract itself from political content.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Journalist says FBI visited after he published hacked Vance document
item.description: An independent journalist says he was visited by the FBI after he published material allegedly obtained through an Iranian hack of former President Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Voter beware: Text scam targets you, no matter where you live
item.description: Reports warn of a scam text falsely claiming that recipients aren’t registered to vote. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals what you need to know and five tips to avoid such schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Corsair upgrades high-end gaming headset with Virtuoso Max Wireless
item.description: Reportedly priced at $329.99, the headset features active noise cancellation, RGB lighting accents and Dolby Atmos support
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: HUD aims to tackle AI skills gap with new assessment approach
item.description: A new skills benchmark model at HUD will assess where employees are in their understanding of AI and recommend a personalized path for training and development. The post HUD aims to tackle AI skills gap with new assessment approach first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Windows users are being tricked by sneaky malware scheme
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how security experts found new malware, Lumma Stealer, spreading via fake verification pages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to leverage APIs for IT-enabled information capability
item.description: In today’s rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses face constant pressure to adapt or risk being left behind. The strategic use of IT-enabled information management capability (IMC) is no longer just an advantage — it’s a necessity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SpaceX catches Starship rocket booster on Texas launchpad
item.description: SpaceX caught a rocket booster on its Texas launchpad as it returned to Earth on Sunday. The chopstick-like arms of the tower, called Mechazilla, grabbed the descending 232-foot booster after a seven-minute flight.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lesser-known xargs command is a versatile time saver
item.description: The xargs command may be one that many Linux users don’t know, but it’s also one that can save a lot of time and trouble on the command line. It’s an unusual command with some very handy uses.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation
item.description: The Pentagon has taken another big step forward to make CMMC a reality. DoD projects the requirements will start showing up in contracts by mid-2025. The post Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
